children.heading: .—Evaluation of benefits and federal investment
children.label: 4 .—Evaluation of benefits and federal investment
children.text: In making a determination for a small start project under paragraph (3)(B), the Secretary shall analyze, evaluate, and consider the following evaluation criteria for the project (as compared to a no-action alternative): mobility improvements, environmental benefits, congestion relief, economic development effects associated with the project, policies and land use patterns that support public transportation and cost-effectiveness as measured by cost per rider.

children.heading: .—Evaluation of local financial commitment
children.label: 5 .—Evaluation of local financial commitment
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (3)(C), the Secretary shall require that each proposed local source of capital and operating financing is stable, reliable, and available within the proposed project timetable.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: In carrying out paragraphs (4) and (5) for a small start project, the Secretary shall evaluate and rate the project on a 5-point scale (high, medium-high, medium, medium-low, or low) based on an evaluation of the benefits of the project as compared to the Federal assistance to be provided and the degree of local financial commitment, as required under this subsection. In rating the projects, the Secretary shall provide, in addition to the overall project rating, individual ratings for each of the criteria established by this subsection and shall give comparable, but not necessarily equal, numerical weight to the benefits that the project will bring to the community in calculating the overall project rating.

children.heading: .—Optional early rating
children.label: B .—Optional early rating
children.text: At the request of the project sponsor, the Secretary shall evaluate and rate the project in accordance with paragraphs (4) and (5) and subparagraph (A) of this paragraph upon completion of the analysis required under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary, to the maximum extent practicable, shall provide Federal assistance under this subsection in a single grant. If the Secretary cannot provide such a single grant, the Secretary may execute an expedited grant agreement in order to include a commitment on the part of the Secretary to provide funding for the project in future fiscal years.

children.heading: .—Terms of expedited grant agreements
children.label: B .—Terms of expedited grant agreements
children.text: In executing an expedited grant agreement under this subsection, the Secretary may include in the agreement terms similar to those established under subsection (k)(2).

children.heading: .—Notice of proposed grants and expedited grant agreements
children.label: C .—Notice of proposed grants and expedited grant agreements
children.text: At least 10 days before making a grant award or entering into a grant agreement for a project under this subsection, the Secretary shall notify, in writing, the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate of the proposed grant or expedited grant agreement, as well as the evaluations and ratings for the project.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: A .—Definition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “future bundling request” means a letter described in subparagraph (B) that requests future funding for additional projects.

children.heading: .—Request
children.label: B .—Request
children.text: When an applicant submits a letter to the Secretary requesting entry of a project into the project development phase under subsection (d)(1)(A)(i)(I), (e)(1)(A)(i)(I), or (h)(2)(A)(i)(I), the applicant may include a description of other projects for consideration for future funding under this section. An applicant shall include in the request the amount of funding requested under this section for each additional project and the estimated capital cost of each project.

children.heading: .—Readiness
children.label: C .—Readiness
children.text: Other projects included in the request shall be ready to enter the project development phase under subsection (d)(1)(A), (e)(1)(A), or (h)(2)(A), within 5 years of the initial project submitted as part of the request.

children.heading: .—Planning
children.label: D .—Planning
children.text: Projects in the future bundling request shall be included in the metropolitan transportation plan in accordance with section 5303(i).

children.heading: .—Project sponsor
children.label: E .—Project sponsor
children.text: The applicant that submits a future bundling request shall be the project sponsor for each project included in the request.

children.heading: .—Program and project share
children.label: F .—Program and project share
children.text: A future bundling request submitted under this paragraph shall include a proposed share of each of the request’s projects that is consistent with the requirements of subsections (k)(2)(C)(ii) or (h)(7), as applicable.

children.label: I
children.text: the total transportation system of the applicant; or

children.label: II
children.text: the transportation system of the region the applicant serves (which, in the case of a State whose request addresses a single region, means that region); and

children.label: I
children.text: streamline procurements for the applicant; or

children.label: II
children.text: enable time or cost savings for the projects.

children.heading: .—Evaluation
children.label: H .—Evaluation
children.text: Each project submitted for consideration for funding in a future bundling request shall be subject to the applicable evaluation criteria under this section for the project type, including demonstrating the availability of local resources to recapitalize, maintain, and operate the overall existing and proposed public transportation system pursuant to subsection (f)(1)(C).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Upon entering into a grant agreement for the initial project for which an applicant submits a future bundling request, the Secretary may issue a letter of intent to the applicant that announces an intention to obligate, for 1 or more additional projects included in the request, an amount from future available budget authority specified in law that is not more than the amount stipulated as the financial participation of the Secretary in the additional project or projects in the future bundling. Such letter may include a condition that the project or projects must meet the evaluation criteria in this subsection before a grant agreement can be executed.

children.heading: .—Amount
children.label: ii .—Amount
children.text: The amount that the Secretary announces an intention to obligate for an additional project in the future bundling request through a letter of intent issued under clause (i) shall be sufficient to complete at least an operable segment of the project.

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: iii .—Treatment
children.text: The issuance of a letter of intent under clause (i) shall not be deemed to be an obligation under sections 1108(c), 1501, and 1502(a) of title 31 or an administrative commitment.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: A .—Definition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “immediate bundling request” means a letter described in subparagraph (B) that requests immediate funding for multiple projects.

children.heading: .—Request
children.label: B .—Request
children.text: An applicant may submit a letter to the Secretary requesting entry of multiple projects into the project development phase under subsection (d)(1)(A)(i)(I), (e)(1)(A)(i)(I), or (h)(2)(A)(i)(I), for consideration for funding under this section. An applicant shall include in the request the amount of funding requested under this section for each additional project and the estimated capital cost of each project.

children.heading: .—Readiness
children.label: C .—Readiness
children.text: Projects included in the request must be ready to enter the project development phase under subsection (d)(1)(A), (e)(1)(A), or (h)(2)(A) at the same time.

children.heading: .—Planning
children.label: D .—Planning
children.text: Projects in the bundle shall be included in the metropolitan transportation plan in accordance with section 5303(i).

children.heading: .—Project sponsor
children.label: E .—Project sponsor
children.text: The applicant that submits an immediate bundling request shall be the project sponsor for each project included in the request.

children.heading: .—Program and project share
children.label: F .—Program and project share
children.text: An immediate bundling request submitted under this subsection shall include a proposed share of each of the request’s projects that is consistent with the requirements of subsections (k)(2)(C)(ii) or (h)(7), as applicable.

children.label: I
children.text: the total transportation system of the applicant; or

children.label: II
children.text: the transportation system of the region the applicant serves (which, in the case of a State whose request addresses a single region, means that region); and

children.label: I
children.text: streamline procurements for the applicant; or

children.label: II
children.text: enable time or cost savings for the projects.

children.heading: .—Evaluation
children.label: H .—Evaluation
children.text: A project submitted for consideration for immediate funding in an immediate bundling request shall be subject to the applicable evaluation criteria under this section for the project type, including demonstrating the availability of local resources to recapitalize, maintain, and operate the overall existing and proposed public transportation system pursuant to subsection (f)(1)(C).

children.heading: Letter of intent or single grant agreement.—
children.label: I Letter of intent or single grant agreement.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Upon entering into a grant agreement for the initial project for which an applicant submits a request, the Secretary may issue a letter of intent or single, combined grant agreement to the applicant.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: A letter of intent announces an intention to obligate, for 1 or more additional projects included in the request, an amount from future available budget authority specified in law that is not more than the amount stipulated as the financial participation of the Secretary in the additional project or projects. Such letter may include a condition that the project or projects must meet the evaluation criteria in this subsection before a grant agreement can be executed.

children.heading: .—Amount
children.label: II .—Amount
children.text: The amount that the Secretary announces an intention to obligate for an additional project in a letter of intent issued under clause (i) shall be sufficient to complete at least an operable segment of the project.

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: III .—Treatment
children.text: The issuance of a letter of intent under clause (i) shall not be deemed to be an obligation under sections 1108(c), 1501, and 1502(a) of title 31 or an administrative commitment.

children.heading: .—Evaluation criteria
children.label: 3 .—Evaluation criteria
children.text: When the Secretary issues rules or policy guidance under this section, the Secretary may request comment from the public regarding potential changes to the evaluation criteria for project justification and local financial commitment under subsections (d), (e), (f), and (h) for the purposes of streamlining the evaluation process for projects included in a future bundling request or an immediate bundling request, including changes to enable simultaneous evaluation of multiple projects under 1 or more evaluation criteria. Notwithstanding paragraphs (1)(H) and (2)(H), such criteria may be utilized for projects included in a future bundling request or an immediate bundling request under this subsection upon promulgation of the applicable rule or policy guidance.

children.heading: .—New start and core capacity improvement projects
children.label: A .—New start and core capacity improvement projects
children.text: A new start project or core capacity improvement project in an immediate bundling request or future bundling request shall be carried out through a full funding grant agreement or expedited grant agreement pursuant to subsection (k)(2).

children.heading: .—Small start
children.label: B .—Small start
children.text: A small start project shall be carried out through a grant agreement pursuant to subsection (h)(7).

children.label: i
children.text: include only projects in an immediate future bundling request that are ready to receive a grant agreement under this section,

children.label: ii
children.text: be carried out through a full funding grant agreement or expedited grant agreement pursuant to subsection (k)(2) for the included projects, if a project seeking assistance under the combined grant agreement is a new start project or core capacity improvement project; and

children.label: iii
children.text: be carried out through a grant agreement pursuant to subsection (h)(7) for the included projects, if the projects seeking assistance under the combined grant agreement consist entirely of small start projects.

children.heading: .—Savings provision
children.label: D .—Savings provision
children.text: The use of a combined grant agreement shall not waive or amend applicable evaluation criteria under this section for projects included in the combined grant agreement.

children.heading: .—Previously Issued Letter of Intent or Full Funding Grant Agreement
children.label: j .—Previously Issued Letter of Intent or Full Funding Grant Agreement
children.text: Subsections (d) and (e) shall not apply to projects for which the Secretary has issued a letter of intent, approved entry into final design, entered into a full funding grant agreement, or entered into a project construction grant agreement before the date of enactment of the Federal Public Transportation Act of 2012.

children.heading: Letters of Intent, Full Funding Grant Agreements, and Early Systems Work Agreements.—
children.label: k Letters of Intent, Full Funding Grant Agreements, and Early Systems Work Agreements.—

children.heading: .—Amounts intended to be obligated
children.label: A .—Amounts intended to be obligated
children.text: The Secretary may issue a letter of intent to an applicant announcing an intention to obligate, for a new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project, an amount from future available budget authority specified in law that is not more than the amount stipulated as the financial participation of the Secretary in the project. When a letter is issued for a capital project under this section, the amount shall be sufficient to complete at least an operable segment.

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: B .—Treatment
children.text: The issuance of a letter under subparagraph (A) is deemed not to be an obligation under sections 1108(c), 1501, and 1502(a) of title 31 or an administrative commitment.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project shall be carried out through a full funding grant agreement.

children.heading: .—Criteria
children.label: B .—Criteria
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into a full funding grant agreement, based on the evaluations and ratings required under subsection (d), (e), or (i), as applicable, with each grantee receiving assistance for a new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project that has been rated as high, medium-high, or medium, in accordance with subsection (g)(2)(A) or (i)(3)(B), as applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: establish the terms of participation by the Government in a new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project;

children.label: ii
children.text: establish the maximum amount of Federal financial assistance for the project;

children.label: iii
children.text: include the period of time for completing the project, even if that period extends beyond the period of an authorization; and

children.label: iv
children.text: make timely and efficient management of the project easier according to the law of the United States.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A full funding grant agreement under this paragraph obligates an amount of available budget authority specified in law and may include a commitment, contingent on amounts to be specified in law in advance for commitments under this paragraph, to obligate an additional amount from future available budget authority specified in law.

children.heading: .—Statement of contingent commitment
children.label: ii .—Statement of contingent commitment
children.text: The agreement shall state that the contingent commitment is not an obligation of the Government.

children.heading: .—Interest and other financing costs
children.label: iii .—Interest and other financing costs
children.text: Interest and other financing costs of efficiently carrying out a part of the project within a reasonable time are a cost of carrying out the project under a full funding grant agreement, except that eligible costs may not be more than the cost of the most favorable financing terms reasonably available for the project at the time of borrowing. The applicant shall certify, in a way satisfactory to the Secretary, that the applicant has shown reasonable diligence in seeking the most favorable financing terms.

children.heading: .—Completion of operable segment
children.label: iv .—Completion of operable segment
children.text: The amount stipulated in an agreement under this paragraph for a new fixed guideway capital project shall be sufficient to complete at least an operable segment.

children.heading: Information collection and analysis plan.—
children.label: E Information collection and analysis plan.—

children.heading: .—Submission of plan
children.label: i .—Submission of plan
children.text: Applicants seeking a full funding grant agreement under this paragraph shall submit a complete plan for the collection and analysis of information to identify the impacts of the new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project and the accuracy of the forecasts prepared during the development of the project. Preparation of this plan shall be included in the full funding grant agreement as an eligible activity.

children.label: I
children.text: collection of data on the current public transportation system regarding public transportation service levels and ridership patterns, including origins and destinations, access modes, trip purposes, and rider characteristics;

children.label: II
children.text: documentation of the predicted scope, service levels, capital costs, operating costs, and ridership of the project;

children.label: III
children.text: collection of data on the public transportation system 2 years after the opening of a new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project, including analogous information on public transportation service levels and ridership patterns and information on the as-built scope, capital, and financing costs of the project; and

children.label: IV
children.text: analysis of the consistency of predicted project characteristics with actual outcomes.

children.heading: .—Collection of data on current system
children.label: F .—Collection of data on current system
children.text: To be eligible for a full funding grant agreement under this paragraph, recipients shall have collected data on the current system, according to the plan required under subparagraph (E)(ii), before the beginning of construction of the proposed new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project. Collection of this data shall be included in the full funding grant agreement as an eligible activity.

children.label: i
children.text: a full funding grant agreement for the project will be made; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the terms of the work agreement will promote ultimate completion of the project more rapidly and at less cost.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: An early systems work agreement under this paragraph obligates budget authority available under this chapter and title 23 and shall provide for reimbursement of preliminary costs of carrying out the project, including land acquisition, timely procurement of system elements for which specifications are decided, and other activities the Secretary decides are appropriate to make efficient, long-term project management easier.

children.heading: .—Contingent commitment
children.label: ii .—Contingent commitment
children.text: An early systems work agreement may include a commitment, contingent on amounts to be specified in law in advance for commitments under this paragraph, to obligate an additional amount from future available budget authority specified in law.

children.heading: .—Period covered
children.label: iii .—Period covered
children.text: An early systems work agreement under this paragraph shall cover the period of time the Secretary considers appropriate. The period may extend beyond the period of current authorization.

children.heading: .—Interest and other financing costs
children.label: iv .—Interest and other financing costs
children.text: Interest and other financing costs of efficiently carrying out the early systems work agreement within a reasonable time are a cost of carrying out the agreement, except that eligible costs may not be more than the cost of the most favorable financing terms reasonably available for the project at the time of borrowing. The applicant shall certify, in a way satisfactory to the Secretary, that the applicant has shown reasonable diligence in seeking the most favorable financing terms.

children.heading: .—Failure to carry out project
children.label: v .—Failure to carry out project
children.text: If an applicant does not carry out the project for reasons within the control of the applicant, the applicant shall repay all Federal grant funds awarded for the project from all Federal funding sources, for all project activities, facilities, and equipment, plus reasonable interest and penalty charges allowable by law or established by the Secretary in the early systems work agreement.

children.heading: .—Crediting of funds received
children.label: vi .—Crediting of funds received
children.text: Any funds received by the Government under this paragraph, other than interest and penalty charges, shall be credited to the appropriation account from which the funds were originally derived.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into full funding grant agreements under this subsection for new fixed guideway capital projects and core capacity improvement projects that contain contingent commitments to incur obligations in such amounts as the Secretary determines are appropriate.

children.heading: .—Appropriation required
children.label: B .—Appropriation required
children.text: An obligation may be made under this subsection only when amounts are appropriated for the obligation.

children.heading: .—Notification to congress
children.label: 5 .—Notification to congress
children.text: Not later than 15 days before issuing a letter of intent, entering into a full funding grant agreement, or entering into an early systems work agreement under this section, the Secretary shall notify, in writing, the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives of the proposed letter or agreement. The Secretary shall include with the notification a copy of the proposed letter or agreement as well as the evaluations and ratings for the project.

children.heading: Government Share of Net Capital Project Cost.—
children.label: l Government Share of Net Capital Project Cost.—

children.heading: .—Estimation of net capital project cost
children.label: A .—Estimation of net capital project cost
children.text: Based on engineering studies, studies of economic feasibility, and information on the expected use of equipment or facilities, the Secretary shall estimate the net capital project cost.

children.heading: .—Grant for new fixed guideway capital project
children.label: i .—Grant for new fixed guideway capital project
children.text: A grant for a new fixed guideway capital project shall not exceed 80 percent of the net capital project cost.

children.heading: .—Full funding grant agreement for new fixed guideway capital project
children.label: ii .—Full funding grant agreement for new fixed guideway capital project
children.text: A full funding grant agreement for a new fixed guideway capital project shall not include a share of more than 60 percent from the funds made available under this section.

children.heading: .—Grant for core capacity improvement project
children.label: iii .—Grant for core capacity improvement project
children.text: A grant for a core capacity improvement project shall not exceed 80 percent of the net capital project cost of the incremental cost to increase the capacity in the corridor.

children.heading: .—Grant for small start project
children.label: iv .—Grant for small start project
children.text: A grant for a small start project shall not exceed 80 percent of the net capital project costs.

children.heading: .—Adjustment for completion under budget
children.label: 2 .—Adjustment for completion under budget
children.text: The Secretary may adjust the final net capital project cost of a new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project evaluated under subsection (d), (e), or (i) to include the cost of eligible activities not included in the originally defined project if the Secretary determines that the originally defined project has been completed at a cost that is significantly below the original estimate.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary determines that the net capital project cost of the project is not more than 10 percent higher than the net capital project cost estimated at the time the project was approved for advancement into the engineering phase; and

children.label: B
children.text: the ridership estimated for the project is not less than 90 percent of the ridership estimated for the project at the time the project was approved for advancement into the engineering phase.

children.label: A
children.text: in cash from non-Government sources;

children.label: B
children.text: from revenues from the sale of advertising and concessions; or

children.label: C
children.text: from an undistributed cash surplus, a replacement or depreciation cash fund or reserve, or new capital.

children.heading: .—Limitation on statutory construction
children.label: 5 .—Limitation on statutory construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as authorizing the Secretary to require a non-Federal financial commitment for a project that is more than 20 percent of the net capital project cost.

children.heading: .—Special rule for rolling stock costs
children.label: 6 .—Special rule for rolling stock costs
children.text: In addition to amounts allowed pursuant to paragraph (1), a planned extension to a fixed guideway system may include the cost of rolling stock previously purchased if the applicant satisfies the Secretary that only amounts other than amounts provided by the Government were used and that the purchase was made for use on the extension. A refund or reduction of the remainder may be made only if a refund of a proportional amount of the grant of the Government is made at the same time.

children.heading: .—Limitation on applicability
children.label: 7 .—Limitation on applicability
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to projects for which the Secretary entered into a full funding grant agreement before the date of enactment of the Federal Public Transportation Act of 2012.

children.heading: .—Special rule for fixed guideway bus rapid transit projects
children.label: 8 .—Special rule for fixed guideway bus rapid transit projects
A
children.text: establish a Government share of at least 80 percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: not lower the project’s rating for degree of local financial commitment for purposes of subsections (d)(2)(A)(v) or (h)(3)(C) as a result of the Government share specified in this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: the State or local governmental authority applies for the payment;

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary approves the payment; and

children.label: C
children.text: before the State or local governmental authority carries out the part of the project, the Secretary approves the plans and specifications for the part in the same way as other projects under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The cost of carrying out part of a project includes the amount of interest earned and payable on bonds issued by the State or local governmental authority to the extent proceeds of the bonds are expended in carrying out the part.

children.heading: .—Limitation on amount of interest
children.label: B .—Limitation on amount of interest
children.text: The amount of interest under this paragraph may not be more than the most favorable interest terms reasonably available for the project at the time of borrowing.

children.heading: .—Certification
children.label: C .—Certification
children.text: The applicant shall certify, in a manner satisfactory to the Secretary, that the applicant has shown reasonable diligence in seeking the most favorable financing terms.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An amount made available or appropriated for a new fixed guideway capital project or core capacity improvement project shall remain available to that project for 4 fiscal years, including the fiscal year in which the amount is made available or appropriated. Any amounts that are unobligated to the project at the end of the 4-fiscal-year period may be used by the Secretary for any purpose under this section.

children.heading: .—Use of deobligated amounts
children.label: 2 .—Use of deobligated amounts
children.text: An amount available under this section that is deobligated may be used for any purpose under this section.

children.heading: Reports on New Fixed Guideway and Core Capacity Improvement Projects.—
children.label: o Reports on New Fixed Guideway and Core Capacity Improvement Projects.—
A
children.text: a proposal of allocations of amounts to be available to finance grants for projects under this section among applicants for these amounts;

children.label: B
children.text: evaluations and ratings, as required under subsections (d), (e), and (i), for each such project that is in project development, engineering, or has received a full funding grant agreement; and

children.label: C
children.text: recommendations of such projects for funding based on the evaluations and ratings and on existing commitments and anticipated funding levels for the next 3 fiscal years based on information currently available to the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: the processes and procedures for evaluating, rating, and recommending all new fixed guideway capital projects and core capacity improvement projects for grant agreements under this section and section 3005(b) of the Federal Public Transportation Act of 2015 ( note; ); and49 U.S.C. 5309Public Law 114–94

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary’s implementation of such processes and procedures;

children.label: I
children.text: a description and analysis of the impacts of the projects on public transportation services and public transportation ridership;

children.label: II
children.text: a description and analysis of the consistency of predicted and actual benefits and costs of the innovative project development and delivery methods of, or innovative financing for, the projects; and

children.label: III
children.text: an identification of the reasons for any differences between predicted and actual outcomes for the projects; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in conducting the review under clause (ii), incorporates information from the plans submitted by applicants under subsection (k)(2)(E)(i); and

children.label: B
children.text: report to Congress on the results of such review by May 31 of the applicable year.

children.heading: .—Special Rule
children.label: p .—Special Rule
children.text: For the purposes of calculating the cost effectiveness of a project described in subsection (d) or (e), the Secretary shall not reduce or eliminate the capital costs of art and non-functional landscaping elements from the annualized capital cost calculation.

children.heading: Joint Public Transportation and Intercity Passenger Rail Projects.—
children.label: q Joint Public Transportation and Intercity Passenger Rail Projects.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may make grants for new fixed guideway capital projects and core capacity improvement projects that provide both public transportation and intercity passenger rail service.

children.heading: .—Eligible costs
children.label: 2 .—Eligible costs
children.text: Eligible costs for a project under this subsection shall be limited to the net capital costs of the public transportation costs attributable to the project based on projected use of the new segment or expanded capacity of the project corridor, not including project elements designed to achieve or maintain a state of good repair, as determined by the Secretary under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: the net capital costs of the public transportation costs attributable to the project as determined under paragraph (4); and

children.label: B
children.text: the share of funds dedicated to the project from sources other than this section included in the unified finance plan for the project.

children.label: A
children.text: engineering studies;

children.label: B
children.text: studies of economic feasibility;

children.label: C
children.text: the expected use of equipment or facilities; and

children.label: D
children.text: the public transportation costs attributable to the project.

children.heading: Government share of net capital project cost.—
children.label: 5 Government share of net capital project cost.—

children.heading: .—Government share
children.label: A .—Government share
children.text: The Government share shall not exceed 80 percent of the net capital cost attributable to the public transportation costs of a project under this subsection as determined under paragraph (4).

children.heading: .—Non-government share
children.label: B .—Non-government share
children.text: The remainder of the net capital cost attributable to the public transportation costs of a project under this subsection shall be provided from an undistributed cash surplus, a replacement or depreciation cash fund or reserve, or new capital.

children.label: A
children.text: Project name.

children.label: B
children.text: Project sponsor.

children.label: C
children.text: City or urbanized area and State in which the project will be located.

children.label: D
children.text: Project type.

children.label: E
children.text: Project mode.

children.label: F
children.text: Project length and number of stops, including length of exclusive bus rapid transit lanes, if applicable.

children.label: G
children.text: Anticipated total project cost.

children.label: H
children.text: Anticipated share of project costs to be sought under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: Date of compliance with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: J
children.text: Date on which the project entered the project development phase.

children.label: K
children.text: Date on which the project entered the engineering phase, if applicable.

children.label: L
children.text: Date on which a Letter of No Prejudice was requested, and date on which a Letter of No Prejudice was issued or denied, if applicable.

children.label: M
children.text: Date of the applicant’s most recent project ratings, including date of request for updated ratings, if applicable.

children.label: N
children.text: Status of the project sponsor in securing non-Federal matching funds.

children.label: O
children.text: Date on which a project grant agreement is anticipated to be executed.

children.heading: .—Updates
children.label: 2 .—Updates
children.text: The Secretary shall update the information provided under paragraph (1) not less frequently than monthly.

children.heading: .—Project profiles
children.label: 3 .—Project profiles
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to make profiles for projects that have applied for or are receiving assistance under this section publicly available in an easily identifiable location on the website of the Department of Transportation, in the same manner as the Secretary did as of the day before the date of enactment of this subsection.
